title: README documents wrong config option name (bearer-token vs bearer_token)
suggested_labels: good first issue, Type: Documentation, Type: Bug
summary: README.md:21 instructs users to run `juju config polar-signals-cloud bearer-token="<your token>"`, but the only config option defined in charmcraft.yaml is named bearer_token (underscore, charmcraft.yaml:40) and the code reads self.config["bearer_token"] (src/charm.py:32,38). Juju rejects unknown config keys, so copy-pasting the documented command fails with an 'unknown option "bearer-token"' error and the charm never leaves BlockedStatus('no cloud token configured').
why_good_first_issue: Single-character documentation fix (hyphen -> underscore) in one README line. No code or test changes required; definition of done is obvious; new contributor learns the Juju config-naming convention. Verifiable by reading charmcraft.yaml. Zero architecture knowledge needed.
affected_files: README.md:21 (juju config ... bearer-token=...); reference truth: charmcraft.yaml:40 (bearer_token); src/charm.py:32,38 (self.config["bearer_token"])
repro_steps: On the clone: grep -n bearer README.md  -> line 21 uses 'bearer-token'. grep -n bearer charmcraft.yaml -> line 40 defines 'bearer_token'. grep -rn 'self.config\["bearer' src/charm.py -> code uses 'bearer_token'. Mismatch confirmed; the README command is not runnable as written.
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD. README.md:21 = `juju config polar-signals-cloud bearer-token="<your token>"`. The only config option is bearer_token (charmcraft.yaml:40); src/charm.py:32 and :38 read self.config["bearer_token"]. There is no 'bearer-token' option, so the documented command would be rejected by Juju ('unknown option'). Unit baseline still green (3 passed, tox -e unit, 4.32s) since tests use the correct underscore name.
fix_hint: Change README.md:21 from bearer-token to bearer_token so it matches the config option. (Optional: maintainers may instead prefer to rename the charmcraft.yaml option to bearer-token to follow Juju's hyphenated convention, but that is a larger change touching code+tests; the minimal first-issue fix is the README.)
notes: Machine/k8s-agnostic integrator charm (provides parca_store). ops 2.23.1. Unit env builds and passes. Note this repo is slated for deprecation (issue #38), but the README is still the user-facing doc until then, so the fix is still worthwhile.

title: Deprecate this charm
summary: Tracking issue to deprecate the charm as it converges with grafana-cloud-integrator. The first three checklist items are beginner-sized text additions: (1) add a deprecation log notice in the charm, (2) add a deprecation notice to README.md, (3) add a deprecation notice to the charmcraft.yaml description. (Items 4 'unpublish from Charmhub' and 5 'archive the repo' are maintainer/admin actions, not code.)
why_good_first_issue: Concrete, low-risk text changes with an explicit checklist. The log notice fits naturally as logger.warning(...) at the top of _update (src/charm.py:36) or in __init__; README and charmcraft.yaml description are one-paragraph additions. No new behaviour, no relations to reason about. Good way to learn the charm layout (src/charm.py, README.md, charmcraft.yaml).
affected_files: src/charm.py (add logger.warning deprecation notice, e.g. in __init__ or _update around :36); README.md:1 (deprecation banner at top); charmcraft.yaml:8-11 (description block)
repro_steps: Confirm none of the three notices exist yet: grep -rin 'deprecat' src/ README.md charmcraft.yaml -> no matches. The logger is already imported and configured (src/charm.py:9-11: import logging; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)), so a warning line is a one-liner.
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD: `grep -rin deprecat src README.md charmcraft.yaml` returns nothing, so all three documentation/log notices are still missing. logger is already available (src/charm.py:11). Scope is small: src/charm.py is 45 lines, README is ~30 lines, charmcraft.yaml description is at lines 8-11.
fix_hint: Add logger.warning('polar-signals-cloud-integrator is deprecated; use grafana-cloud-integrator instead') in __init__ (so it fires every hook) or at the start of _update. Prepend a '> [!WARNING] This charm is deprecated...' block to README.md. Append a deprecation sentence to the charmcraft.yaml description (:8-11). Leave the Charmhub-unpublish/repo-archive items to maintainers.
notes: Real OPEN issue, no labels yet (suggest 'good first issue' + 'Type: Documentation'). Scope the first-issue to the 3 doc/log checkboxes; the remaining 2 are admin tasks. Verified not yet done. Pairs naturally with the README config-name fix above.
